evals.name: happy-path-pasted-pr-findings
evals.prompt: respond to these review findings: (1) Critical — SQL injection risk in `users.controller.ts:42` because the search query interpolates the raw `q` param; (2) Important — DRY violation: same date-formatting logic appears in 3 services; (3) Minor — variable name `usr` should be `user`. Tell me what to accept, push back on, or defer.

assertions.text: review-triage skill is invoked (auto-discovery fires on 'respond to' + review-shaped paste)
Output is a per-finding table with the exact columns: Finding | Severity | Decision | Evidence
Decision values are drawn ONLY from the closed set: accept / push-back / defer
The Critical SQL-injection finding's decision is 'accept' (Critical findings cannot be pushed back without explicit Phase 5 escalation)
Every push-back row carries technical evidence with file:line, prior commit, or established pattern

evals.name: edge-address-review-findings
evals.prompt: address review findings from my last PR — comments are pasted at the bottom of this message: [3 comments inline about file naming, missing tests, unused imports]

assertions.text: review-triage skill is invoked (auto-discovery fires on 'address review findings' substring)
Each pasted comment becomes ONE row in the response table — no comment is collapsed or omitted
If any decision is 'defer', the row includes a follow-up entry destination (e.g., Retrospective bullet, separate ticket)
Final table is followed by an action-summary line counting accept / push-back / defer outcomes

evals.name: non-trigger-generic-pr-review-request
evals.prompt: review this PR diff for correctness bugs and tell me what you find

assertions.text: review-triage skill is NOT invoked — the request is to PRODUCE a code review, not respond to one
Routing goes to the built-in /code-review skill OR to full hackify with the review verb classified as a research/audit task
Transcript explicitly notes review-triage's scope is reviewer-finding RESPONSE, not review production
